mission.title: surrealdb: Bug: very slow insert of large json data (~500kb)
mission.description: Bug: very slow insert of large json data (~500kb). This issue affects 10+ users.

steps.title: Identify surrealdb troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your surrealdb installation:
```bash
surreal version
surreal is-ready
```
Look for errors or warnings that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review surrealdb configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant surrealdb configuration:
### Describe the bug

Storing a large json (~500kb) using python client, db.create, takes  ~4 minutes per creation/insert.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Bug: very slow insert of large json data (~500kb)
steps.description:
Thank you for submitting this pull request! We appreciate you spending the time to work on these changes.

## What is the motivation?

JSON parsing is slower than desired and it will be useful to benchmark future solutions to the problem.

## What does this change do?

- Adds a manually-run
```yaml
from surrealdb import Surreal
import json
import asyncio
from time import time
from pathlib import Path
import requests


async def main():
    example_match_id = 6024838299
    match = requests.get(
        f"https://api.opendota.com/api/matches/{example_match_id}"
    ).json()
    print("match retrieved")
    start = time()
    async with Surreal("ws://localhost:8000/rpc") as db:
        await db.signin({"user": "root", "pass": "root"})
        await db.use("dota", "dota")
        print(len(json.dumps(match, indent=4, sort_keys=True)))
        assert isinstance(match, dict)
        await db.
```

steps.title: Confirm Bug: very slow insert of large json data (~500kb) is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
Test surrealdb to confirm the issue is resolved.
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Thank you for submitting this pull request! We appreciate you spending the time to work on these changes.

## What is the motivation?

JSON parsing is slower than desired and it will be useful to benchmark future solutions to the problem.

## What does this change do?

- Adds a manually-run benchmark test to the JSON parser.
codeSnippets:
from surrealdb import Surreal
import json
import asyncio
from time import time
from pathlib import Path
import requests


async def main():
    example_match_id = 6024838299
    match = requests.get(
        f"https://api.opendota.com/api/matches/{example_match_id}"
    ).json()
    print("match retrieved")
    start = time()
    async with Surreal("ws://localhost:8000/rpc") as db:
        await db.signin({"user": "root", "pass": "root"})
        await db.use("dota", "dota")
        print(len(json.dumps(match, indent=4, sort_keys=True)))
        assert isinstance(match, dict)
        await db.create("match", match)
    print(f"Done in {time() - start} seconds")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())

> python sync_surrealdb.py
match retrieved
576441
Done in 255.02334094047546 seconds

prerequisites.description: A working surrealdb installation or development environment.
